perturbation.original_text: 8. Upon written request of a Party who has disclosed Proprietary Information to a receiving Party, the receiving Party shall promptly return all Proprietary Information except that one copy may be retained by legal counsel of the receiving Party as evidence of what was disclosed.
perturbation.changed_text: 8. Upon written request of a Party who has disclosed Proprietary Information to a receiving Party, the receiving Party may choose whether or not to return all Proprietary Information except that multiple copies may be retained by any employee of the receiving Party for any purpose.
perturbation.explanation: The perturbation in paragraph 8 introduces a contradiction with paragraph 4 and 10. The original text mandates the prompt return of Proprietary Information upon request, with only one copy retained by legal counsel. The changed text allows the receiving party to choose whether to return the information and allows multiple copies to be retained by any employee for any purpose, violating the confidentiality terms established in paragraph 4 and also violating the conditions of agreement termination in paragraph 10.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4. Except as provided in Paragraphs 6 and 7 hereof, Proprietary Information disclosed to a receiving Party shall for a period of one (1 ) year from the effective date of this Agreement be held in confidence by the receiving Party and not be disclosed to others or used except for the purposes set forth above, without the prior written approval of the disclosing Party.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in paragraph 8 directly contradicts paragraph 4. Paragraph 4 states that Proprietary Information shall be held in confidence and not disclosed, while the changed text in paragraph 8 allows the receiving party to choose whether to return the information and allows multiple copies to be retained by any employee for any purpose. This contradicts the confidentiality requirement in paragraph 4, creating a direct conflict regarding the handling and disclosure of Proprietary Information. The changed text weakens the obligation to protect Proprietary Information, directly clashing with the requirement in paragraph 4 to hold it in confidence and not disclose it. Therefore, it creates an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 10. This Agreement shall be e ffective on the date of its full execution by the Parties. a. This Agreement shall terminate at the end of the period of years as provided for in paragraph 4 above, or upon the delivery of written notice of termination by a Party to the other Party; however, the obligations of a receiving Party pursuant to Paragraph 4 shall remain in effect for the term specified therein. b. Notwithstanding the termination of this agreement, the supplied data must be maintained and protected in accordance with its provisions fo r three (3) years following the termination of this agreement. At the conclusion of this agreement/contract for which data is exchanged, proprietary data shall be returned to the provider or destroyed with a certification to that effect provided to the ot her party, except the one (1) copy retained by legal counsel as provided in paragraph 8 above.
perturbation.changed_text: 10. This Agreement shall be effective on the date of its full execution by the Parties. a. This Agreement shall terminate immediately upon written notice of termination by a Party to the other Party; and the obligations of a receiving Party pursuant to Paragraph 4 will also terminate immediately. b. Notwithstanding the termination of this agreement, the supplied data must be maintained and protected in accordance with its provisions for five (5) years following the termination of this agreement. At the conclusion of this agreement/contract for which data is exchanged, proprietary data may be retained indefinitely by the receiving party without any obligation to return or destroy it.
perturbation.explanation: This perturbation creates several inconsistencies. First, it states the agreement terminates immediately upon written notice, contradicting the earlier duration of one year of Paragraph 4. Second, the obligations under Paragraph 4, which includes maintaining confidentiality, are now terminated immediately, which contradicts maintaining the supplied data for five years following the agreement. Finally, there is in-text contradiction on what should happen with the proprietary data, after the termination.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4. Except as provided in Paragraphs 6 and 7 hereof, Proprietary Information disclosed to a receiving Party shall for a period of one (1 ) year from the effective date of this Agreement be held in confidence by the receiving Party and not be disclosed to others or used except for the purposes set forth above, without the prior written approval of the disclosing Party.
perturbation.justification: The changed text states that the agreement terminates immediately upon written notice, which directly contradicts Paragraph 4, stating that Proprietary Information should be held in confidence for one (1) year from the effective date. The changed text also allows proprietary data to be retained indefinitely, directly contradicting the requirement to return or destroy it, as stipulated in the original text.

perturbation.original_text: 11. Except as specifically provided herein, neither Party makes any warranty, express or implied, oral or written, with respect to products, services, or information supplied hereunder, including any warranties related to patent, trademark, or copyright infringements. In no event shall either Party be liable to the other for indirect, special, consequential, punitive, exemplary, or incidental damages including, but n ot limited to damages for loss of use of facilities or equipment, loss of revenue, loss of profits or loss of goodwill regardless of (a) the negligence (either sole or concurrent) of either party; and (b) whether either party has been informed of the possi bility of such damages.
perturbation.changed_text: 11. Except as specifically provided herein, Excelerate makes no warranty, express or implied, oral or written, with respect to products, services, or information supplied hereunder, including any warranties related to patent, trademark, or copyright infringements. In no event shall Excelerate be liable to the other for indirect, special, consequential, punitive, exemplary, or incidental damages including, but not limited to damages for loss of use of facilities or equipment, loss of revenue, loss of profits or loss of goodwill regardless of (a) the negligence (either sole or concurrent) of either party; and (b) whether either party has been informed of the possibility of such damages. However, the Second Party fully warrants the accuracy and reliability of all information provided and shall be liable for all damages resulting from inaccuracies.
perturbation.explanation: The modification introduces a contradiction by specifying that only Excelerate makes no warranties, while the Second Party fully warrants its information and is liable for inaccuracies. This contradicts the first part of the paragraph by creating unequal warranty obligations, and creating legal uncertainty for the responsibilities for each party, with the information they are providing.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 11. Except as specifically provided herein, Excelerate makes no warranty, express or implied, oral or written, with respect to products, services, or information supplied hereunder, including any warranties related to patent, trademark, or copyright infringements. In no event shall Excelerate be liable to the other for indirect, special, consequential, punitive, exemplary, or incidental damages including, but not limited to damages for loss of use of facilities or equipment, loss of revenue, loss of profits or loss of goodwill regardless of (a) the negligence (either sole or concurrent) of either party; and (b) whether either party has been informed of the possibility of such damages. However, the Second Party fully warrants the accuracy and reliability of all information provided and shall be liable for all damages resulting from inaccuracies.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies section 11 to state that 'Excelerate makes no warranty,' but 'the Second Party fully warrants the accuracy and reliability of all information provided and shall be liable for all damages resulting from inaccuracies.' This directly contradicts the original intent of the clause, which was that neither party makes any warranties. The contradicted text is identical to the changed text where the change was made. The change causes a direct contradiction by imposing warranty obligations on one party while explicitly excluding them for the other, therefore it is a strong in-text contradiction. It fits the 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' definition.

perturbation.original_text: 14. The parties acknowledge and agree that due to the unique nature of the Proprietary Information, and breach of this agreement by the receiving party would cause irreparable harm to th e disclosing party and that the disclosing party shall therefore be entitled to equitable relief in addition to all other remedies at law. The parties agree that, in the event of breach, or threatened breach of the terms of this agreement, the originating party may seek an injunction prohibiting such breach. Both parties hereby waiver any requirement to post bond for attempts to obtain such injunctive relief. Any such relief shall be in addition to and not in lieu of any appropriate relief in the way of m onetary damages. Each party shall pay its own attorney’s fees, costs, and expenses for any dispute under the agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 14. Only Excelerate acknowledges and agrees that due to the unique nature of the Proprietary Information, and breach of this agreement by the receiving party would cause irreparable harm to the disclosing party and that the disclosing party shall therefore be entitled to equitable relief in addition to all other remedies at law. Only Excelerate agrees that, in the event of breach, or threatened breach of the terms of this agreement, the originating party may seek an injunction prohibiting such breach. Only Excelerate waives any requirement to post bond for attempts to obtain such injunctive relief. Any such relief shall be in addition to and not in lieu of any appropriate relief in the way of monetary damages. Each party shall pay its own attorney’s fees, costs, and expenses for any dispute under the agreement.
perturbation.explanation: This change creates an in-text contradiction by specifying that only Excelerate acknowledges the irreparable harm from a breach, agrees to injunctive relief, and waives the bond requirement. This creates imbalance in the agreement, and introduces ambiguity about the Second Party's obligations and rights in case of a breach, leading to an in-text contradiction and dispute.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 14. Only Excelerate acknowledges and agrees that due to the unique nature of the Proprietary Information, and breach of this agreement by the receiving party would cause irreparable harm to the disclosing party and that the disclosing party shall therefore be entitled to equitable relief in addition to all other remedies at law. Only Excelerate agrees that, in the event of breach, or threatened breach of the terms of this agreement, the originating party may seek an injunction prohibiting such breach. Only Excelerate waives any requirement to post bond for attempts to obtain such injunctive relief. Any such relief shall be in addition to and not in lieu of any appropriate relief in the way of monetary damages. Each party shall pay its own attorney’s fees, costs, and expenses for any dispute under the agreement.
perturbation.justification: The change introduces an in-text contradiction. The original text states that 'The parties acknowledge and agree...' indicating a mutual agreement between both parties. The changed text modifies this to state 'Only Excelerate acknowledges and agrees...', limiting the agreement to only one party. This creates an imbalance in the agreement and contradicts the notion of mutual obligation and agreement, leading to a dispute. The contradicted text is exactly the same as the changed text, but both create a strong in-text contradiction. Therefore, this constitutes a strong in-text contradiction based on the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' because the different sections (original vs changed) now provide conflicting obligations, leading to ambiguity about the Second Party's obligations and rights in case of a breach.
